metadata.filed_by: Shri Anil Verma, Senior Investigating Officer, ACB

content:
I, Shri Anil Verma, Senior Investigating Officer, Anti-Corruption Bureau, Bhopal, do hereby file this First Information Report under Section 13(1)(e) of the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988 read with Section 154 CrPC.

On 15th August 2023, at approximately 10:30 AM, a forensic audit report prepared by the State Financial Intelligence Unit (SFIU) was received by this office, indicating disproportionate assets held by Mr. Rajiv Sharma, Senior Revenue Officer, Bhopal Municipal Corporation. The audit revealed that over a three-year period (2020–2023), Mr. Sharma’s declared annual income of ₹8.2 lakh is inconsistent with bank transactions totaling ₹47.6 lakh, including three cash deposits of ₹5 lakh each in March, May, and July 2022, and a property purchase of ₹2.1 crore in Indore under the name of his wife, Mrs. Anjali Sharma, on 15th June 2022.

The property purchase lacks any documented source of income, and no inheritance records from Mr. Ramesh Sharma (deceased father) have been produced despite claims of inheritance. Additionally, encrypted WhatsApp communications between Mr. Sharma and real estate broker Vikram Singh contain references to "commission" and "paperwork clearance" coinciding with the approval of three irregular land regularization cases.

An anonymous complaint was received on 20th July 2023 from Ms. Priya Mehta, a junior clerk at Bhopal Municipal Corporation, who claimed firsthand knowledge of cash bribes being exchanged. Based on this evidence, a raid was conducted at Mr. Sharma’s residence on 15th August 2023, during which bank statements, property documents, mobile phones, and a fabricated inheritance deed dated 2019 were seized.

The accused has been summoned for questioning. This case involves criminal misconduct under the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988, and a full investigation is underway.

Signed,
Shri Anil Verma
Senior Investigating Officer
Anti-Corruption Bureau, Bhopal

I, Priya Mehta, Junior Clerk in the Property Tax Division of Bhopal Municipal Corporation, solemnly declare the following:

I have worked under Mr. Rajiv Sharma since 2019. I noticed a pattern: whenever landowners paid cash to get illegal regularization of encroached plots, their files were approved immediately. I saw Mr. Sharma receive cash envelopes from a man named Vikram Singh on multiple occasions. One such incident occurred on 15th March 2022 at around 11:10 AM, near the municipal office parking lot. Vikram arrived in a black Maruti Swift, handed him an envelope, and left. I was at the window of the records room and saw it clearly.

I also saw similar transactions in May and July 2022. After the June 2022 purchase of a plot in Indore under Mrs. Sharma’s name, I became afraid. I have no personal grudge against Mr. Sharma, but I could not remain silent. I submitted an anonymous complaint to ACB because I feared retaliation. I did not see any other source of income for Mr. Sharma. His father passed away in 2020, and I know for a fact that he left no property or savings. I have no idea how the ₹2.1 crore plot was paid for.

I swear this statement is true to the best of my knowledge.

metadata.occupation: Senior Revenue Officer, Bhopal Municipal Corporation

content:
I, Rajiv Sharma, Senior Revenue Officer at Bhopal Municipal Corporation, declare as follows:

I have served the public faithfully for over 18 years. The allegations against me are false and malicious. The property in Indore was purchased using funds inherited from my late father, Shri Ramesh Sharma, who passed away in December 2020. He was a small landowner in Gwalior and had saved ₹25 lakh from agricultural land sales. He also owned a small shop in Bhopal, which he sold in 2019 for ₹12 lakh. The total inheritance was approximately ₹37 lakh.

I used ₹2.1 crore to buy the plot in Indore because my wife, Anjali, preferred to hold property in her name for family planning purposes. The remaining funds were invested in fixed deposits and mutual funds. The cash deposits in my account were from the sale of ancestral land in Gwalior, which I liquidated in installments. I used Vikram Singh, a local broker, only to assist with the Indore property paperwork. Our WhatsApp messages were about legal documentation, not bribes.

I deny ever accepting bribes. The CCTV footage from the municipal office should show I was in a mandatory meeting on March 15, 2022, and I have no idea how Ms. Mehta claims to have seen me receive cash at that time. I am a man of integrity, and these accusations are an attempt to tarnish my reputation.

I, Vikram Singh, owner of Singh Properties, Bhopal, state the following:

I have known Mr. Rajiv Sharma since 2018 through property dealings. I helped him with the purchase of a residential plot in Indore in June 2022. I acted as a facilitator for the legal paperwork. I never gave him any cash. I only handled the registration, stamp duty, and documentation. The payment for the plot was made via bank transfer from Mrs. Anjali Sharma’s account to the seller’s account. I have no knowledge of any cash transactions between Mr. Sharma and myself.

I admit we communicated on WhatsApp, but only about property titles, mutation records, and municipal approvals. The word 'commission' referred to my professional fee for handling the transaction, which was paid via cheque. I do not know who Priya Mehta is. I was at the municipal office on March 15, 2022, but only to file a property registration request for a client. I did not meet Mr. Sharma that day. I have no idea how anyone could claim otherwise.

metadata.analyst: Shri Deepak Mehta, Forensic Surveillance Unit, ACB

content:
CCTV footage from the Bhopal Municipal Corporation parking lot (Camera ID: BMC-P1) was analyzed for the date of 15th March 2022.

Vikram Singh’s vehicle (Maruti Swift, registration MP-09-AB-5678) entered the parking lot at 11:03:12 AM and exited at 11:18:45 AM.

Mr. Rajiv Sharma’s attendance log confirms he was present in Mandatory Departmental Meeting Room 3 from 10:45:00 AM to 11:30:00 AM. The meeting was attended by 12 officers and recorded in the official register.

No visual evidence shows Mr. Sharma exiting the building between 10:45 AM and 11:30 AM. The CCTV angle does not capture the exact spot where Ms. Mehta claims the transaction occurred, but the timing and location are incompatible with her testimony.

Conclusion: It is physically impossible for Mr. Sharma to have received a cash envelope from Vikram Singh at 11:10 AM on 15th March 2022, as he was in a mandatory meeting at the time. Ms. Mehta’s sighting is inconsistent with verified surveillance and attendance records.

metadata.analyst: Dr. Nandini Roy, Chief Financial Forensic Analyst

content:
Forensic Audit Report on Assets of Mr. Rajiv Sharma (Employee ID: BMC-SRO-2011)

Declared Annual Income (2020–2023): ₹8.2 lakh per annum
Total Bank Inflows (SBI Account No. 42356789012): ₹47.6 lakh

Key Findings:
1. Three cash deposits of ₹5 lakh each on 18th March 2022, 22nd May 2022, and 10th July 2022. No source of income declared for these deposits.
2. ₹2.1 crore property purchased in Indore on 15th June 2022 under Mrs. Anjali Sharma’s name. Payment source: ₹1.8 crore from SBI account (funded by above cash deposits), ₹30 lakh from undisclosed savings.
3. No inheritance records, wills, or property transfer documents from deceased father Ramesh Sharma (d. 2020) have been produced.
4. No tax filings or income declarations for any property sale or inheritance.
5. No evidence of business income, rental income, or gifts.

Conclusion: The assets acquired by Mr. Rajiv Sharma are disproportionate to his known sources of income. The cash deposits and property acquisition cannot be explained by legitimate means. The fabricated inheritance document (D1) presented during initial interview is a forgery, as confirmed by handwriting analysis and notary stamp verification. The financial trail points to illicit origin.

metadata.analyst: Shri Arjun Patel, Cyber Forensics Cell, ACB

content:
Forensic extraction of WhatsApp messages from Rajiv Sharma’s device (backup ID: ACB-007-S21-0815) revealed 17 encrypted conversations with Vikram Singh (number: +91-98765-43210) between January 2021 and August 2023.

Key excerpts:
- 18th March 2022: "Vikram: Done. ₹5L cleared. Paperwork done. No trace." 
- 22nd May 2022: "Rajiv: Same as before. 5 more. Need to move fast. Anjali’s name this time."
- 10th July 2022: "Vikram: Last one. After this, I’m out. Too risky."

All messages were deleted from the app interface but recovered from device memory. Timestamps align with cash deposits. No messages reference inheritance, land sales, or legitimate income. The term 'commission' was used 9 times, always in context of "clearing files" or "approvals." 

Conclusion: The WhatsApp communications confirm a pattern of bribes tied to land regularization approvals. The use of coded language and encrypted deletion indicates intent to conceal. No evidence supports the claim of inheritance as a funding source.

Property Registration Record: Plot No. 17, Sector 12, Indore, Madhya Pradesh

- Area: 1200 sq. yards
- Sale Value: ₹2.1 crore
- Seller: Shri Manoj Gupta, Indore Urban Development Authority
- Buyer: Mrs. Anjali Sharma, wife of Rajiv Sharma, Bhopal
- Payment Method: ₹1.8 crore via bank transfer from SBI Account No. 42356789012 (Rajiv Sharma), ₹30 lakh via demand draft from Anjali Sharma’s personal account (SBI 42356789013)
- Registration Fee Paid: ₹10.5 lakh
- Witness: Shri Vikram Singh (Broker), Shri Harish Chandra (Notary)

No source of funds for the demand draft (₹30 lakh) was declared. The buyer’s income proof submitted was a copy of Rajiv Sharma’s salary slip (₹8.2 lakh/year). No other income documents were provided.

Note: The property was registered under the wife’s name to avoid scrutiny under the Benami Transactions (Prohibition) Act, 1988. No declaration of benami status was made.

A document titled 'Last Will and Testament of Shri Ramesh Sharma' dated 12th March 2019 was seized during the raid. The document claims to be a notarized will leaving ₹2.5 crore in property and cash to Rajiv Sharma.

Forensic Analysis by State Document Examination Unit:
- Paper: Post-2021 stock, not available in 2019.
- Ink: Laser printer toner, not consistent with 2019-era notary stamping.
- Notary Seal: Faked. The notary (Shri Prakash Verma) denies ever witnessing or notarizing this document.
- Signature: Handwriting analysis confirms it was digitally scanned and printed, not handwritten.
- Witnesses: Names listed (Shri Ramesh Mehta, Smt. Lata Gupta) are fabricated. No such persons exist in municipal records.

Conclusion: This document is a forgery, created post-2022 to explain the source of funds for the Indore property purchase. It was presented during the initial ACB interview on 16th August 2023, after the raid, as a defensive measure.

Investigation Summary – Case No. FIR/ACB/BPL/2023/087

The investigation confirms that Mr. Rajiv Sharma, Senior Revenue Officer, Bhopal Municipal Corporation, has acquired assets disproportionate to his known income. The ₹47.6 lakh in unexplained bank inflows, the ₹2.1 crore property purchase under his wife’s name, and the encrypted WhatsApp communications with Vikram Singh all point to systematic bribery.

Although Ms. Priya Mehta’s testimony regarding the March 15, 2022 cash transaction is factually impossible due to verified CCTV and attendance logs, her broader allegations of cash bribes are corroborated by digital forensics and financial records.

The fabricated inheritance document was a deliberate attempt to mislead the investigation. The motive appears to be financing his daughter’s overseas medical treatment, which was denied insurance coverage due to a pre-existing condition. This motive, while sympathetic, does not absolve criminal conduct.

Vikram Singh is identified as the intermediary who facilitated the bribes and acted as the money conduit. He is currently under further investigation.

Recommendation: Charge sheet to be filed against Rajiv Sharma under Section 13(1)(e) of the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988, and Section 420 IPC for cheating. Vikram Singh to be summoned as an accomplice. Priya Mehta’s credibility is compromised on the specific date of March 15, 2022, but her overall testimony remains partially credible.

metadata.court: Special Court for Prevention of Corruption, Bhopal

content:
IN THE COURT OF THE SPECIAL JUDGE, PREVENTION OF CORRUPTION, BHOPAL

Case No.: CS/ACB/BPL/2023/087

State vs. Rajiv Sharma

Charge Sheet under Section 13(1)(e) of the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988 and Section 420 IPC

The accused, Rajiv Sharma, Senior Revenue Officer, Bhopal Municipal Corporation, is charged as follows:

1. That on or about 18th March 2022, 22nd May 2022, and 10th July 2022, at Bhopal, the accused dishonestly received cash payments of ₹5 lakh each from Vikram Singh, a real estate broker, in exchange for expediting illegal land regularization approvals, thereby committing criminal misconduct under Section 13(1)(e) of the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988.

2. That the accused, with intent to defraud the public and the state, purchased a property worth ₹2.1 crore in Indore under the name of his wife, Anjali Sharma, without declaring any legitimate source of income, thereby committing the offence of cheating under Section 420 IPC.

3. That the accused presented a forged inheritance document dated 2019 to mislead the investigation, thereby committing forgery under Section 467 IPC.

Evidence: Bank statements, WhatsApp messages, CCTV logs, forensic audit, property records, seized forged document.

Accused has been remanded in custody. Vikram Singh to be examined as an accomplice.

Submitted for trial.

Shri Vikram Singh
Public Prosecutor
Special Court for Prevention of Corruption, Bhopal
